title: AI can build and maintain software at a professional level
description: An AI system can implement, debug, refactor, architect, and sustain software work at a level useful to experienced engineers.

subQuestions.title: Can it write code that passes hidden tests?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether generated code generalizes beyond visible examples and public benchmark contamination.
subQuestions.rationale: Strong code models now solve a large share of hidden-test implementation tasks across common software domains.

proofItems.title: Benchmark suite: Hidden-test software implementation
proofItems.shortExplanation: Contamination-resistant coding suites show strong hidden-test pass rates on many focused implementation tasks.

subQuestions.title: Can it debug unfamiliar repositories?
subQuestions.description: Tests whether the system can localize faults and patch them in codebases it has not seen before.
subQuestions.rationale: Repository debugging is improving quickly, but still struggles on architecture-heavy bugs and subtle multi-file regressions.

proofItems.title: Benchmark suite: Debugging unfamiliar repositories
proofItems.shortExplanation: Repository debugging benchmarks show strong progress, but still leave meaningful gaps on dependency and state-management failures.

subQuestions.title: Can it refactor without breaking behavior?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether code changes preserve external behavior instead of only improving local style or structure.
subQuestions.rationale: Refactoring quality is often good when tests are strong, but behavior-preserving change remains unreliable on messier codebases.

proofItems.title: Deployment audit: Behavior-preserving refactor safety
proofItems.shortExplanation: Code audits show useful cleanup and modularization, but still document regression risk on poorly covered systems.

subQuestions.title: Can it design reasonable architectures for real requirements?
subQuestions.description: Tests whether the system proposes structures that reflect real tradeoffs, not only textbook decompositions.
subQuestions.rationale: Architecture proposals are often credible for small and mid-sized systems, though still need human judgment for long-term fit.

proofItems.title: Expert blind review: Architecture quality under real product constraints
proofItems.source: Expert Review Panel on AI Performance
proofItems.shortExplanation: Senior engineer review panels judged many generated architectures workable, but not yet consistently strong on deeper platform tradeoffs.

subQuestions.title: Can it ship and maintain a working small application?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether the system can carry a real app through build, release, and follow-up maintenance loops.
subQuestions.rationale: Shipping small apps is feasible in demos, but dependable release hardening and maintenance still require heavy human ownership.

proofItems.title: Deployment audit: Small-app shipping and maintenance
proofItems.shortExplanation: Real project audits show promising build completion, but still document repeated human intervention at deployment and maintenance stages.
